description:
Execute a shell command and return its output (stdout + stderr combined).
- You MUST set workdir explicitly; never rely on the current process directory
- Commands time out after 120s by default
- Common uses: run EditMode tests, show git status, find asmdef references
- python/pip use the Python runtime selected in Settings > General; managed Python installs pip packages into the Locus data directory
- Use the description field to briefly describe what the command does
- PATH and environment variables are refreshed from the system on every call — CLIs installed by a previous command are directly available without PATH tweaks
- stdin is closed: a command that prompts for input fails or hangs until timeout. Set interactive=true ONLY when the user must type into a real terminal (login/auth wizards, password/2FA/device-code prompts, TTY-only CLIs); it opens a visible terminal window on the user's machine, output is NOT captured and only the exit code is returned
- After an interactive command, verify the outcome with a non-interactive check instead of trusting the exit code; if it fails before the user can interact (e.g. exit code 127), rerun with interactive=false to capture the error, fix it, then retry interactively

description.description: Brief description of what this command does, e.g. 'Run EditMode tests', 'Show git status', 'Find asmdef references'

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds (default 120000, or 600000 when interactive=true)

interactive.description: Run the command in a visible terminal window where the user types input directly (login wizards, password prompts, TTY-only CLIs). Output is not captured; only the exit code is returned. Default false

workdir.description: Working directory to run the command in. Required.
